faqs.question: How do I know if I have carpenter ants in my Bulls Head home?
faqs.answer: Signs of carpenter ants include piles of wood shavings (frass) near wooden structures, rustling sounds inside walls, and sightings of large black ants, especially during spring months. Bulls Head homes with wooden siding or decks are particularly susceptible. Our technicians provide free inspections to accurately identify carpenter ant activity and assess the extent of infestation.

faqs.question: What determines the cost of carpenter ant control in Bulls Head?
faqs.answer: Pricing ranges from $350 for minor, localized infestations to $2,000 for severe cases requiring extensive treatment and structural repairs. Factors include the size of your property, number of colonies present, extent of wood damage, and accessibility of nesting sites. We provide upfront estimates after a thorough inspection of your Bulls Head property.

faqs.question: How long does carpenter ant treatment take in Bulls Head homes?
faqs.answer: Initial treatment typically takes 1-2 hours depending on property size and infestation severity. Most Bulls Head homes require follow-up visits at 2-week intervals to ensure complete colony elimination. You'll see significant reduction in ant activity within 48-72 hours, with full eradication usually achieved within 4-6 weeks.
